2026-04-15T22:04:48.7233345Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7233903Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7234315Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7234674Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7234969Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7235197Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7235349Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7235521Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7235686Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-15T22:04:48.7235909Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-15T22:04:48.7237074Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-15T22:07:07.9411906Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T22:05:50.849913951Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58187,\"title\":\"[Identity] Docs: Azure.Identity.Broker README outdated after 1.6.0 release\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Identity.Broker`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (version increment after release of Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nAzure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 was released on 2026-04-14 with a critical dependency change: the package now depends on `Azure.Core` directly (not `Azure.Identity`), as the Azure.Identity types moved to Azure.Core in the 1.53.0 type consolidation. Additionally, several features added across versions 1.3.0–1.6.0 remain undocumented in the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Outdated Prerequisites section**: Still lists `Azure.Identity` as a prerequisite, but since 1.6.0 the package depends on `Azure.Core` directly. The README should also call out the critical compatibility requirement: version 1.6.0+ is required when using `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ (or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+), with a link to the [migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility).\\n- **Missing \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section**: The README has no \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section, which is required by the standard README template. There are no sample test files under `tests/Samples/` either. Concrete code examples for creating `InteractiveBrowserCredential` with a parent window handle (HWND) are needed.\\n- **Cross-platform support not documented**: Version 1.3.0 (stable) added macOS and Linux/WSL broker support, but the README only mentions Windows (WAM) implicitly. Platform requirements and limitations should be documented.\\n- **DI/Configuration integration undocumented**: Version 1.4.0 added experimental `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` integration (with link to configuration docs), but the README doesn\\u0026#39;t mention it.\\n- **JSON schema / appsettings.json IntelliSense undocumented**: Version 1.5.0 added a JSON schema segment enabling IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`, but the README doesn\\u0026#39;t mention it.\\n- **No `samples/` directory**: Unlike comparable packages, there are no standalone sample files for this package.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md`:\\n\\n1. **Update Prerequisites section** — replace the existing `Azure.Identity` prerequisite entry:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Prerequisites\\n\\n   * An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].\\n   * The [Azure.Core][azure_core] package (1.53.0 or later, included transitively via `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+).\\n\\n   \\u0026gt; **Important:** Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 or later is required when your application references `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+. Earlier versions of this package are incompatible with the type consolidation introduced in those releases. See the [migration guide][broker_migration] for details.\\n   ```\\n\\n   Update the link references at the bottom of the README to add:\\n   ```markdown\\n   [azure_core]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md\\n   [broker_migration]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Add \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — insert between the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Troubleshooting\\u0026#34; sections:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ## Examples\\n\\n   ### Authenticate with InteractiveBrowserCredential using a broker\\n\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n   The snippet content will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after writing the sample test file in Step 2.\\n\\n3. **Add cross-platform support note** in the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Platform support\\n\\n   The authentication broker is supported on:\\n   - **Windows**: Windows Authentication Manager (WAM) via the built-in broker.\\n   - **macOS**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0).\\n   - **Linux and WSL**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). Prerequisites and setup instructions are described in [Enable SSO in native Linux apps using MSAL.NET]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Add DI/Configuration integration note** in the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Configuration and dependency injection\\n\\n   This package supports optional integration with `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`. For details, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) documentation.\\n\\n   A JSON schema is included in the NuGet package to provide IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`.\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/tests/Samples/Sample1_InteractiveBroker.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.Identity.Broker;\\nusing Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.Identity.Broker.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_InteractiveBroker\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;This test is a live test that requires user interaction.\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void InteractiveBrowserWithBroker()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser\\n            // Obtain the parent window handle for your application.\\n            // On a WinForms or WPF application, use the handle of the main form/window.\\n            // The value IntPtr.Zero instructs the broker to select an appropriate parent window automatically.\\n            IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero;\\n\\n            var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(\\n                new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle));\\n\\n            var client = new SecretClient(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;This test is a live test that requires user interaction.\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void InteractiveBrowserWithBrokerUseDefaultAccount()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount\\n            // Set UseDefaultBrokerAccount to true to silently use the currently signed-in\\n            // operating system account, bypassing the interactive sign-in prompt.\\n            IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero;\\n\\n            var options = new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle)\\n            {\\n                UseDefaultBrokerAccount = true\\n            };\\n\\n            var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(options);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n- In the README, insert the following placeholder in the new \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section for the first snippet:\\n\\n  ````markdown\\n  ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser\\n  ```\\n  ````\\n\\n  And a second example for default account:\\n\\n  ````markdown\\n  ### Authenticate with the signed-in OS account (silent)\\n\\n  ```C# Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount\\n  ```\\n  ````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md` currently has these sections:\\n\\n| # | Section | Status |\\n|---|---------|--------|\\n| 1 | Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate) | ⚠️ Prerequisites outdated |\\n| 2 | Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing platform support and DI/config notes |\\n| 3 | Examples | ❌ Missing entirely |\\n| 4 | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| 5 | Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| 6 | Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — this reads `#region Snippet:` tags from test files and injects the code into README.md placeholder blocks. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — regenerates public API listing files. Required if any public types or members were added or changed.\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/` — ensures consistent code formatting.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above.\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24480182032/agentic_workflow) · ● 401.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58187\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-15T22:02:25Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-15T22:02:25Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Additionally, several features added across versions 1.3.0–1.6.0 remain undocumented in the README.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Outdated Prerequisites section**: Still lists `Azure.Identity` as a prerequisite, but since 1.6.0 the package depends on `Azure.Core` directly. The README should also call out the critical compatibility requirement: version 1.6.0+ is required when using `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ (or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+), with a link to the [migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility).\\n- **Missing \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section**: The README has no \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section, which is required by the standard README template. There are no sample test files under `tests/Samples/` either. Concrete code examples for creating `InteractiveBrowserCredential` with a parent window handle (HWND) are needed.\\n- **Cross-platform support not documented**: Version 1.3.0 (stable) added macOS and Linux/WSL broker support, but the README only mentions Windows (WAM) implicitly. Platform requirements and limitations should be documented.\\n- **DI/Configuration integration undocumented**: Version 1.4.0 added experimental `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` integration (with link to configuration docs), but the README doesn\\u0026#39;t mention it.\\n- **JSON schema / appsettings.json IntelliSense undocumented**: Version 1.5.0 added a JSON schema segment enabling IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`, but the README doesn\\u0026#39;t mention it.\\n- **No `samples/` directory**: Unlike comparable packages, there are no standalone sample files for this package.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/README.md`:\\n\\n1. **Update Prerequisites section** — replace the existing `Azure.Identity` prerequisite entry:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Prerequisites\\n\\n   * An [Azure subscription][azure_sub].\\n   * The [Azure.Core][azure_core] package (1.53.0 or later, included transitively via `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+).\\n\\n   \\u0026gt; **Important:** Azure.Identity.Broker 1.6.0 or later is required when your application references `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+ or `Azure.Identity` 1.21.0+. Earlier versions of this package are incompatible with the type consolidation introduced in those releases. See the [migration guide][broker_migration] for details.\\n   ```\\n\\n   Update the link references at the bottom of the README to add:\\n   ```markdown\\n   [azure_core]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md\\n   [broker_migration]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md#azureidentitybroker-compatibility\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Add \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section** — insert between the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Troubleshooting\\u0026#34; sections:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ## Examples\\n\\n   ### Authenticate with InteractiveBrowserCredential using a broker\\n\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n   The snippet content will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after writing the sample test file in Step 2.\\n\\n3. **Add cross-platform support note** in the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Platform support\\n\\n   The authentication broker is supported on:\\n   - **Windows**: Windows Authentication Manager (WAM) via the built-in broker.\\n   - **macOS**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0).\\n   - **Linux and WSL**: Microsoft broker (added in 1.3.0). Prerequisites and setup instructions are described in [Enable SSO in native Linux apps using MSAL.NET]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n   ```\\n\\n4. **Add DI/Configuration integration note** in the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n   ```markdown\\n   ### Configuration and dependency injection\\n\\n   This package supports optional integration with `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration` and `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection`. For details, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) documentation.\\n\\n   A JSON schema is included in the NuGet package to provide IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json`.\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**Create** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity.Broker/tests/Samples/Sample1_InteractiveBroker.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. requires user interaction.\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void InteractiveBrowserWithBroker()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_InteractiveBrowser\\n            // Obtain the parent window handle for your application.\\n            // On a WinForms or WPF application, use the handle of the main form/window.\\n            // The value IntPtr.Zero instructs the broker to select an appropriate parent window automatically.\\n            IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero;\\n\\n            var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(\\n                new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle));\\n\\n            var client = new SecretClient(new Uri(\\u0026#34;(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;This test is a live test that requires user interaction.\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public void InteractiveBrowserWithBrokerUseDefaultAccount()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Identity_Broker_UseDefaultBrokerAccount\\n            // Set UseDefaultBrokerAccount to true to silently use the currently signed-in\\n            // operating system account, bypassing the interactive sign-in prompt.\\n            IntPtr parentWindowHandle = IntPtr.Zero;\\n\\n            var options = new InteractiveBrowserCredentialBrokerOptions(parentWindowHandle)\\n            {\\n                UseDefaultBrokerAccount = true\\n            };\\n\\n            var credential = new InteractiveBrowserCredential(options);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n- In the README, insert the following placeholder in the new \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section
